qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing bounds check in double-far pointer resolution allows arbitrary memory access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a separate double-far pointer bounds-check bypass in capnp/src/private/layout.rs::follow_fars(); the advisory root cause is safe exposure of constant::Reader and Raw/StructSchema construction paths that rely on get_root_unchecked.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns safe Rust APIs constant::Reader::get and StructSchema/RawStructSchema construction reaching get_root_unchecked with unvalidated words or encoded schema data. The only final qualifying finding concerns an unrelated missing bounds_check for the second pointer in double-far pointer resolution.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5w5r-mf82-595p describes UB in constant::Reader::get and StructSchema::new caused by unchecked construction over arbitrary words or schemas through PointerReader::get_root_unchecked.
Advisory fix 7b981f4c75a975c80444cd38729bcdf12bf3eabf makes constant::Reader fields pub(crate) and adds an unsafe constructor for raw word-backed constants.
Advisory fix e3aeec213e6d1b30a182bf61682a370f20d8a02c changes generated RawStructSchema construction to unsafe RawStructSchema::new calls.

double_far_bounds_bypass/vulnerability_report.json identifies capnp/src/private/layout.rs:536-541 and 571-577, specifically missing validation of far_position_in_segment for the second pointer in double-far follow_fars()/zero_object() handling.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists double_far_bounds_bypass as the only final qualifying_vuln finding.
